Typical range for Nd:YAG lasers in stone cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for light contamination on Onyx (chalcedony-based, low thermal conductivity); max safe below 500W to prevent cracking (based on IPG Photonics specs and studies in J. Cult. Herit. 2018).

Effective speeds for contamination removal; 500 mm/s recommended for optimal balance on Onyx; slower (100-500 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster (500-2000 mm/s) for light; from industry manuals like those from Adapt Laser Systems.

Nanosecond regime typical for industrial cleaning to minimize heat-affected zone (HAZ) on heat-sensitive Onyx; 20 ns recommended over ps/fs for cost-effectiveness while reducing thermal accumulation (laser physics in Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020).
Common for 1064 nm cleaning; 30 kHz recommended for Onyx to balance speed and heat buildup (high freq risks accumulation on low-conductivity substrates); trade-off noted in Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019.
Fluence range for safe contaminant ablation; Onyx damage threshold ~6-8 J/cm² (SiO2-based); 1.5 J/cm² recommended for cleaning without substrate etching (ablation studies in J. Appl. Phys. 2017).
Beam diameter for uniform coverage; 300 μm recommended for Onyx surface preparation (fine detail vs. large area); from equipment specs like Coherent lasers, suitable for stone texturing.

Typically 1-3 passes for complete removal on stones; 2 passes recommended for Onyx to ensure uniformity without overexposure; based on case studies in industrial cleaning (Laser Cleaning Handbook, 2021).
Ensures uniform coverage; 50% recommended for Onyx to avoid gaps or excessive overlap-induced damage; typical range from process optimization in Opt. Express 2016.
